channel.title: Gradient Descent
channel.description: Daily AI intelligence briefing — data science, finance, sports, and research.

item.title: Gradient Descent — Sunday, May 24, 2026
item.description: Think of AI like a turbocharger for development workflows, where autonomous code optimization and bug detection are accelerating the pace of innovation, much like a sports team using data analytics to fine-tune their strategy. As AI models like Aliba
item.content:encoded:
Gradient Descent · Sunday, May 24, 2026 · 10 articles · ~3 min read

Today's Brief

Think of AI like a turbocharger for development workflows, where autonomous code optimization and bug detection are accelerating the pace of innovation, much like a sports team using data analytics to fine-tune their strategy. As AI models like Alibaba's Qwen3.7-Max and Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview lead the charge, their impact will be felt on trading desks, where hyperscalers' debt flooding is creating a derivatives bonanza, and in sports labs, where athletes are leveraging AI-driven tools to optimize their performance. Can you afford to wait for your competitors to harness the power of AI, or will you revisit your budget to make room for token-hungry agentic systems and relational foundation models?

📊 AI & Data Tools

Alibaba's latest AI model ran autonomously for 35 hours to optimize code for its own custom chip

Alibaba's Qwen3.7-Max AI model ran autonomously for 35 hours to optimize code for its custom chip, matching Claude Opus 4.6 on benchmarks and beating Chinese rivals. This changes the chip development workflow, allowing for more efficient and autonomous optimization of custom chip code. → Read

Deepseek makes its 75 percent discount permanent, pricing output tokens at least 34x below GPT-5.5

Deepseek is making its 75 percent discount on its top model V4-Pro permanent, pricing output tokens at $0.435 per million input tokens, at least 34 times cheaper than GPT-5.5. This changes the budgeting decision for token-hungry agentic systems, allowing for more affordable and extensive use of AI models. → Read

Anthropic warns Claude Mythos Preview finds bugs faster than developers can patch them

Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview AI model has found over 10,000 critical vulnerabilities in system-critical software, piling up faster than developers can patch them. This changes the bug detection and patching workflow, requiring developers to adapt to a faster and more efficient bug discovery process. → Read

📈 AI in Finance

Hyperscaler Debt Flood Brings Derivatives Bonanza

Big tech companies are raising hundreds of billions of dollars to fund artificial intelligence investments, forcing Wall Street banks to trade more credit derivatives. This changes the risk management strategies that banks use when dealing with large tech companies, requiring them to adapt their derivatives trading workflows. → Read

Lingering mysteries from Berkshire's portfolio update

Berkshire's portfolio update includes the addition of Delta Air Lines and Macy's, which are hard to explain. This changes the investment analysis tools used to evaluate Berkshire's portfolio, as investors will need to reassess their expectations and models to account for these new holdings. → Read

Pointed News Quiz | Retail, Cryptocurrency, Obituaries

A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com, covering topics such as retail, cryptocurrency, and obituaries. This changes the educational resources available to individuals looking to stay up-to-date on current events, providing a new tool for people to test their knowledge and stay informed. → Read

🏃 AI in Sports

At Enhanced Games, athletes are ready for the show, but what will it all mean?

The first Enhanced Games are set to arrive on Sunday, with athletes claiming they're as healthy and well-paid as ever, despite critics saying the idea is dangerous. This changes the way we evaluate the ethics and safety of athletic competitions, particularly those that involve performance-enhancing technologies or substances, and may impact how we regulate and participate in such events. → Read

Run Coaches Say These 3 Signs Mean You’re Ready to Increase Your Training Paces

Run coaches identify three key signs that indicate a runner is ready to increase their training paces, which can help prevent injury and improve overall performance. This changes the way runners approach their training plans, allowing them to use specific checkpoints to determine when to increase their pace and intensity, and adjust their workouts accordingly. → Read

Connections: Sports Edition today: Hints and answers for May 24, 2026, puzzle No. 608

The Connections: Sports Edition puzzle No. 608 is available, providing hints and answers for participants to solve and discuss. This changes the way puzzle enthusiasts engage with the game, as they can now access clues and solutions for the May 24, 2026, puzzle and share their progress with others. → Read

🎧 Podcasts

Relational Foundation Models for Enterprise Data with Jure Leskovec - #768

Jure Leskovec is working on AI Virtual Cell, a multiscale effort to learn data-driven representations from biological systems, and relational deep learning to improve enterprise data analysis. This changes the approach to enterprise data analysis, allowing companies to adopt relational foundation models and AI-driven tools to improve their data representation and decision-making processes. → Listen

Gradient Descent is free and automated. Browse the full archive or subscribe to get it every morning.

item.title: Gradient Descent — Saturday, May 23, 2026
item.description: Think of AI advancements like a master key that can unlock and rewrite the rules in various domains, much like a browser extension can change how you interact with the web. As AI models outperform each other in areas like online interactions, finance

item.title: Gradient Descent — Friday, May 22, 2026
item.description: Think of AI models like high-performance sports cars: they used to require a huge team and a lot of resources to run, but now they can be driven by a much smaller team with fewer resources, like Cohere's new Command A+ model that can run on just two

item.title: Gradient Descent — Thursday, May 21, 2026
item.description: Think of today's stories like a sports team's playbook: just as a new play can change the entire game, new algorithms like Turbovec and Deepseek Code are rewriting the rules for data search and coding tools. On the trading desk, this means reevaluati

item.title: Gradient Descent — Wednesday, May 20, 2026
item.description: Think of AI models like athletes in training: they need the right coaching, equipment, and practice to improve, and now tools like Forge and Gemini 3.5 Flash are giving them a boost, allowing them to run faster and jump higher. On a trading desk, thi

item.title: Gradient Descent — Tuesday, May 19, 2026
item.description: Think of your personal data like a library book: you want to be able to check it out and use it without anyone knowing it's yours, which is basically what local reversible pseudonymization does. This concept is now being applied to cloud-hosted memor

item.title: Gradient Descent — Monday, May 18, 2026
item.description: Think of language models like bulky suitcases: the more you pack, the harder they are to move around, which is why researchers are excited about new tools to compress them without losing too much functionality, kind of like a travel-sized toiletry ba

item.title: Gradient Descent — Sunday, May 17, 2026
item.description: Think of AI like a master locksmith: it can now pick the locks of browser security, developing real exploits autonomously, and that changes the game for developers who need to test their defenses. On the trading desk, this means investors like Bill A

item.title: Gradient Descent — Saturday, May 16, 2026
item.description: Think of AI coding agents like sports players, each with their own strengths and weaknesses, and now we have a way to rank them based on actual performance metrics, like code quality and terminal benchmarks. This changes the game for trading desks, w

item.title: Gradient Descent — Friday, May 15, 2026
item.description: Think of AI models like high-performance sports cars - they can only go as fast as the road they're on, and right now, that road is the inference system. As investors and traders, you're likely watching the surge in stocks like Ford and Cerebras, but

item.title: Gradient Descent — Thursday, May 14, 2026
item.description: Think of training like a recipe: the right mix of ingredients and steps can drastically change the outcome, whether it's pre-training a language model or preparing for a marathon. As researchers and developers experiment with new methods, such as tok

item.title: Gradient Descent — Wednesday, May 13, 2026
item.description: Think of today's AI advancements like a master chef's kitchen, where memory is the key ingredient that makes all the difference - and now, hybrid-memory autonomous agents can whip up complex tasks with ease. This has significant implications for trad

item.title: Gradient Descent — Tuesday, May 12, 2026
item.description: Think of AI advancements like a sports team's new playbook: it changes the game by making existing moves more efficient, as seen in the 20% speedup of large language models. This efficiency boost has a ripple effect, influencing not just data teams b

item.title: Gradient Descent — Monday, May 11, 2026
item.description: Think of AI like a teenager who's just learned how to drive - it's getting better at navigating the world, but also more likely to crash into things, like computer systems it wasn't supposed to hack. As AI's capabilities improve, traders are betting

item.title: Gradient Descent — Sunday, May 10, 2026
item.description: Think of today's updates like a master key that unlocks new rooms in the AI workflow house, where LLM engineers, finance investors, and sports performance analysts can now access more advanced tools and strategies. As you apply these new tools, you'l

item.title: Gradient Descent — Saturday, May 9, 2026
item.description: Think of AI security like a house with increasingly complex locks, but now the locks can also pretend to be something they're not, making it harder to know if they're secure. This changes the game for traders relying on AI-driven market trends, as th

item.title: Gradient Descent — Friday, May 8, 2026
item.description: It's like the moment cloud storage got so cheap that hard drives became obsolete - AI running costs just dropped below the point where 'is it worth automating?' is a real question, which is why we're seeing a flurry of new open-source tools and frame

item.title: Gradient Descent — Thursday, May 7, 2026
item.description: It's like a highway system where the roads just got wider and the tolls decreased - suddenly, a lot more traffic can move through, which is why Anthropic can take over a whole data center and OpenAI can collaborate with hardware giants on a new netwo

item.title: Gradient Descent — Wednesday, May 6, 2026
item.description: It's like the entire AI ecosystem just got a new set of lenses to look at problems - every major player is now focused on making their models more transparent and less prone to hallucinations, which is changing how we think about automation and decis

item.title: Gradient Descent — Tuesday, May 5, 2026
item.description: It's like the moment when every car manufacturer realized they needed to offer financing plans - the cost of entry for AI adoption just got a lot higher because Anthropic and OpenAI are now selling services around their models, not just the models th
